audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for third visit warning, test yourselves, weakness/power of Messiah, restoration prayer, final appeals and blessing.

verses.restored: This third time I am coming to you. Upon the mouth of two witnesses and three every word will stand.
I have said beforehand and I say beforehand, as when present the second time and now absent, to those who sinned/misaligned before and to all the rest, that if I come again I will not spare,
since you seek proof of the Messiah speaking in me, who is not weak toward you but is powerful among you.
For indeed he was crucified from weakness, but he lives from power of the Divine. For we also are weak in him, but we will live with him from power of the Divine toward you.
Test yourselves, whether you are in the trust; examine yourselves. Or do you not recognize yourselves, that Jesus Messiah is in you? Unless indeed you are disapproved.
But I hope you will know that we are not disapproved.
Now we pray to the Divine that you do nothing harmful; not so that we may appear approved, but so that you may do the beautiful/good, even if we may be as disapproved.
For we are not able to do anything against the truth, but for the truth.
For we rejoice whenever we are weak and you are powerful; this also we pray, your restoration/completion.
Because of this I write these things while absent, so that when present I may not act sharply according to the authority that the Lord gave me for building up and not for tearing down.
Finally, siblings, rejoice, be restored, be encouraged/comforted, think the same, live in peace; and the Divine of love and peace will be with you.
Greet one another with a holy kiss.
All the holy ones greet you.
The grace of the Lord Jesus Messiah and the love of the Divine and the fellowship/participation of the holy Spirit be with you all.

translation_notes: Authority is again explicitly for building up, not tearing down.
Restoration/completion in verse 9 and final exhortation in verse 11 frame the whole severe section as repair, not punishment for its own sake.
